---
title: "About these notes"
---

## Scope

This is the preface of the example book. Replace it with a short
paragraph describing the course, the lecturer, the semester, and the
textbook references.

## How to read

Each chapter is built around one or more lectures. Definitions,
theorems, propositions, examples, and exercises live in styled callout
boxes so the reader can scan a chapter quickly.

## Notation

Set the conventions for the rest of the book here — for instance:

- Random variables in upper case ($X$, $Y$); realisations in lower case.
- Vectors and matrices in bold ($\boldsymbol{x}$, $\boldsymbol{\theta}$,
  $\mathbf{X}$).
- $\log = \ln$ unless stated otherwise.

## Sources

List the textbooks and lecture notes the chapters draw on. Every chapter
should end with its own *Sources* section pointing to the specific
chapters and slide decks it covers.
